deployment_question: How does the model transfer to a biological assay or assay context absent from training?
definition: Hold out all measurements from one or more assays, assay families, protocols, or biological contexts to test assay transfer rather than compound-level interpolation.

split_operator.spectra_mapping: sets same-assay train overlap to zero for held-out assays

implementation_recipe: Define assays or assay contexts before splitting.
Assign complete assays or assay families to test.
Report compound overlap and assay-context similarity separately.
quality_gates: Distinguish compound novelty from assay novelty.
Check whether compounds from held-out assays appear in training assays.
Report assay label prevalence and measurement protocol differences.
failure_modes: Assay metadata can be noisy or incomplete.
A held-out assay may differ in target, protocol, and label distribution simultaneously.
Repeated compounds can make the split easier than true assay transfer.

citations.title: Toward Assay-Aware Bioactivity Model(er)s
citations.evidence: Shows assay context matters for protein-ligand interaction modeling and can be encoded with assay descriptors.

citations.title: Benchmarking compound activity prediction for real-world drug discovery applications
citations.evidence: Reports variable model performance across assays in a compound activity prediction benchmark.
